title: Why NASA Studies This Man (He Attracts UFOs!)
summary: Chris Bledsoe discusses his UFO experiences beginning in 2007, his interactions with NASA mission controller Tim Taylor, and connections to mid-century anti-gravity inventor Thomas Townsen Brown. The conversation explores exotic metal recovery, consciousness-based phenomena, and Bledsoe's ongoing role as a 'super experiencer' studied by government scientists.

acronyms.definition: Genetic material that carries hereditary information
Energy molecule in cells, mentioned in context of hemoglobin and sun energy

organizations_mentioned: University of North Carolina Chapel Hill

events.description: Chris Bledsoe's initial UFO encounter while fishing in North Carolina on January 8th
University of North Carolina Chapel Hill physics patrons allegedly sent science down the wrong path with quantum gravity
Thomas Townsen Brown's anti-gravity experiments reportedly worked in Paris at the Montgolfier facility
Chris Bledsoe received a message about divine encoding in music and prayed to tell certain musicians about it

key_claims.claim: NASA mission controller Tim Taylor tested the Bledsoe family with exotic metal that caused Chris to have a physical reaction indicating he had been taken aboard a craft
Tim Taylor claimed to work for 'the hammer' who works for God, referencing concepts from the film The Adjustment Bureau
Tim Taylor was part of a secret time travel group called the Nassau Group with Thomas Townsen Brown as president
Chris Bledsoe and Tim Taylor recovered exotic metal in Zanesville, Ohio, the birthplace of Thomas Townsen Brown
Hal Puthoff received a piece of the recovered metal from Zanesville
Thomas Townsen Brown's daughter Linda Brown was helped by Tim Taylor in writing her book
Tim Taylor told Bledsoe that Towns and Brown received visits from small beings who landed in his yard and gave him information
Chris Bledsoe worked with Vivx Biotechnologies drawing diagrams of cells based on intuitive downloads for stem cell research

When placed in a Faraday cage at the Monroe Institute, Bledsoe could still sense UFOs above the building, which scientists interpreted as proof the phenomenon is consciousness-based rather than electromagnetic
The exotic metal acts like an animal, always seeking energy and trying to connect, and bonds to DNA through frequency
There are over 200 mainstream scientists who want to start working on the UFO phenomenon
Thomas Townsen Brown essentially discovered anti-gravity in the 1950s
University of North Carolina Chapel Hill was deeply involved in anti-gravity research according to declassified documents
